title: tanacetum balsamita var. balsamitoides
intro: Tanacetum balsamita var. balsamitoides, also known as Balsam Tansy, is a perennial herb of the Asteraceae family, characterized by its distinctive scent and clusters of small, yellowish flowers. The plant's medicinal properties include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Balsam Tansy include its ability to ease digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
